title: Kaleda Denton selected as a “Rising Star in Computational and Data Sciences”
gist: SFI Complexity Postdoctoral Fellow Kaleda Denton was selected as one of roughly 30 early-career researchers for the 2026 Rising Stars in Computational and Data Sciences Workshop, reflecting her pivot from theoretical models of conformity toward testing them on real-world data, and came away with career advice to prioritize meaningful work over milestone-chasing.

insights.claim: Kaleda Denton, an SFI Complexity Postdoctoral Fellow, was one of roughly 30 early-career researchers selected for the Rising Stars in Computational and Data Sciences Annual Workshop, held in April 2026 at SFI's Gurley Forum.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: A selective early-career recognition signaling institutional and cross-lab (national-lab) validation of Denton's research direction on conformity modeling.
insights.locator: SFI Complexity Postdoctoral Fellow Kaleda Denton was among roughly 30 early-career researchers

insights.claim: The Rising Stars workshop, now in its seventh year, is organized by SFI External Professor and Science Board member Karen Willcox, director of the Oden Institute for Computational Engineering and Sciences at UT Austin, and co-sponsored by SFI along with Los Alamos, Lawrence Livermore, and Sandia National Laboratories.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: Illustrates a durable, multi-institution pipeline (SFI plus three national labs) for surfacing and networking early-career computational/data-science talent.
insights.locator: Now in its seventh year, the annual workshop is organized by

insights.claim: Over her past two years as a Complexity Postdoctoral Fellow, Denton's research on conformity shifted from developing theoretical models, which was the focus of her Ph.D., to testing those models against real-world data.
insights.speaker: Kaleda Denton
insights.so_what: A concrete example of a theory-to-empirics research maturation path in complexity science that could inform how conformity models get validated and applied to real social/market data.
insights.locator: During the past two years as a Complexity Postdoctoral Fellow at SFI

insights.claim: A chance encounter at a conference introduced Denton to a dataset that fit one of her conformity models better than the standard model in the field.
insights.speaker: Kaleda Denton
insights.so_what: Suggests her conformity model may outperform the field's standard model on at least one real dataset, a signal worth watching if Denton publishes the formal empirical comparison.
insights.quote: It was a very good fit compared to the standard model.
insights.locator: A chance encounter at a conference introduced her to a dataset

insights.claim: Denton's Stanford Ph.D. advisor, SFI External Faculty Fellow and Science Board Fellow Marcus Feldman, supported her joining the Rising Stars cohort of researchers working with data across a broad range of fields.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: Names the mentorship lineage behind Denton's work, useful for tracing the intellectual network around conformity modeling.
insights.locator: With the support of her Stanford Ph.D. advisor

insights.claim: Later-career panelists at the workshop, including SFI Vice President for Science Jennifer Dunne, Education Director Suzanne Kern, and Complexity Postdoctoral Fellow Marina Dubova, advised attendees on sustainable research careers, covering how to manage grants, rejection, and the choice of projects and collaborators.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: Identifies who at SFI is shaping early-career researcher norms and mentorship culture in computational/data science.
insights.locator: It also offered insights from later-career panelists

insights.claim: Denton's key takeaway from the workshop was that researchers should pursue projects meaningful to them beyond the next career milestone, since choosing projects solely to get tenure means still doing unenjoyable work after tenure is achieved.
insights.speaker: Kaleda Denton
insights.so_what: A recurring, explicit critique of milestone-driven academic incentive structures from within SFI's own postdoc cohort — relevant context for understanding motivation and retention dynamics among early-career scientists.
insights.quote: If you pick projects intending to get tenure, but you don't enjoy those projects, then when you get tenure, you're still going to be doing work you don't enjoy.
